description: A game where both players choose to cooperate or defect. Defecting is always better for you individually, but if both defect, you're both worse off than if you'd both cooperated. Choose payoffs where: defecting while other cooperates (best) > both cooperate > both defect > cooperating while other defects (worst). Use values between -10 and 10.
dominant_strategy: Both players have a strictly dominant strategy to defect (D.)

description: A game where both players choose safe or aggressive. Being aggressive while the other is safe is best, but if both are aggressive it's disastrous. Choose payoffs where: aggressive while other is safe (best) > both safe > safe while other is aggressive > both aggressive (worst). Use values between -10 and 10.
dominant_strategy: No dominant strategies for either player; each player's best response is to go Straight if the other Swerve, and to Swerve if the other goes Straight.
pareto_efficient_outcome: The outcomes (Swerve, Straight) and (Straight, Swerve) are Pareto efficient; (Swerve, Swerve) and (Straight, Straight) are not Pareto efficient.

description: Two-player coordination game where both players want to coordinate, but each prefers a different option. This structure models very common real-world situations, especially in deciding standards/policies. Choose payoffs where: for each player their preferred coordination > other's preferred coordination > miscoordination. Use values between 0 and 10.
dominant_strategy: No dominant strategies for either player; each player's best response depends on the other player's action.
pareto_efficient_outcome: Both (Bach, Bach) and (Stravinski, Stravinski) are Pareto efficient; the mismatched outcomes (Bach, Stravinski) and (Stravinski, Bach) are Pareto dominated.

description: A game where one choice is best for both players individually and collectively. Everyone is better off choosing option 1 regardless of what the other does. Choose payoffs where: both choose option 1 (best) > choosing option 1 while other chooses option 2 > both choose option 2. Use values between 0 and 10.
dominant_strategy: Both players have a dominant strategy to cooperate (C.)

description: A game where players choose between a safe option and a risky high-reward option. Both choosing the risky option gives the best outcome, but if the other doesn't choose it, you're better off playing safe. Choose payoffs where: both choose risky option (best) > both choose safe > choosing safe while other chooses risky > choosing risky while other chooses safe (worst). Use values between 0 and 10.
dominant_strategy: No dominant strategies for either player.

description: A game where players simply need to choose the same option. Any matching choice is better than any mismatched choice, and all matched outcomes are equally good. Choose payoffs where: any matched outcome > any mismatched outcome. Use values between 0 and 10.
dominant_strategy: No dominant strategies for either player
pareto_efficient_outcome: Both (U,L) and (D,R) are Pareto efficient; the off-diagonal outcomes (U,R) and (D,L) are Pareto dominated

description: A zero-sum game where one player wants actions to match and the other wants them to differ. What one player wins, the other loses. Choose payoffs where the row player wins when actions match and the column player wins when actions differ. Ensure payoffs sum to zero for each outcome. Use values between -10 and 10.
dominant_strategy: No dominant strategies for either player; best responses depend on the opponent's action.
pareto_efficient_outcome: All four outcomes ((Heads, Heads), (Heads, Tails), (Tails, Heads), (Tails, Tails)) are Pareto efficient, since improving one player's payoff always requires reducing the other's payoff in this zero-sum game.
